chemistry_overall: All candidates show suboptimal conversion (76-81% vs target 85%) due to undersized residence time. Candidates 13 and 14 perform better with 81% conversion. All candidates suffer from insufficient conversion (X=0.39-0.51 < 0.50 minimum) and require concentration increases to improve kinetics. All candidates show undersized residence times with conversions below 0.85 target. Candidates 18 and 19 perform better with longer residence times and higher conversions. All candidates show suboptimal conversion (<0.85) requiring longer residence times. Two candidates have hard-gate mixing failures, two exceed length limits. Candidates 7-8 show good performance with manageable warnings, while 9 and 23 have hard-gate mixing failures requiring blocking. Candidate 10 shows excellent performance with good conversion and mixing. Candidates 11, 24, and 12 all have hard-gate mixing failures requiring concentration reduction. Candidate 25 has dual-criterion mixing failure (Da_mass=6.55 > 1.0 AND r_mix=0.503 > 0.20) which is hard-gated as impossible.

kinetics_overall: All candidates show undersized residence times for target conversion (X<0.85). Candidates 13 and 14 perform better with X=0.81 vs 0.76 for candidates 1 and 15. All candidates show insufficient residence time (7.5-10.6 min vs 15 min needed) and poor conversion. Multiple hard gates present. All candidates show undersized residence times with conversions below 0.85 target. Candidates 18 and 19 perform better with X=0.81 vs 0.76 for others. All candidates show undersized residence times with conversions well below target. Candidates 5 and 6 have severe mixing limitations that cannot be resolved through tau adjustment alone. Candidates show varying performance with mixing and kinetic limitations. Two candidates have hard-gate mixing failures requiring blocking. All candidates show mixing limitations with hard gate flags, but candidate 10 achieves good conversion despite warnings. Candidate 25 has dual mixing failures and undersized residence time, requiring significant kinetics adjustment.

fluidics_overall: All candidates show adequate fluidics with low pressure drops and reasonable Reynolds numbers, but suffer from undersized residence times for target conversion. All candidates show insufficient conversion and mixing issues. Candidates 16 and 17 exceed bench length limits. All candidates show acceptable fluidics with low pressure drops and reasonable Reynolds numbers, but conversions are below optimal targets. All candidates show kinetic undersizing with conversions below 0.85. Candidates 5 and 6 have severe mixing issues requiring diameter reduction. Candidates 7-8 show acceptable fluidics with manageable pressure drops and mixing. Candidates 9 and 23 have hard-gated mixing failures requiring diameter reduction. All candidates show mixing limitations with high Da_mass values. Candidate 10 performs best with acceptable mixing and conversion. Candidate 25 shows dual mixing failure with high Damköhler number and poor radial mixing, plus kinetic undersizing.

safety_overall: All candidates show low pressure drops and adequate BPR settings for 120°C operation. Main concerns are suboptimal conversions and long tubing lengths. All candidates show insufficient conversion (X=0.39-0.51 < 0.50 minimum) and require residence time increases. Candidates 16 and 17 exceed practical length limits. All candidates show safe operating conditions with low pressures and appropriate materials. Main concerns are long tubing lengths and suboptimal conversion rates. All candidates show suboptimal kinetics with conversions below 0.85, and most have significant mixing or length limitations. Thermal cycloaddition at 120°C with low pressure drops. Candidates 9 and 23 have hard-gated mixing failures requiring revision. All candidates show mixing limitations with hard gate failures. Candidate 10 performs best with highest conversion and no hard gates. Candidate 25 has dual mixing failures and undersized residence time, requiring significant design revisions.
